flow: field_importance: innate/adaptive context without disease laundry list
clinical_translation_gap: why mouse-only work fails for human pathways
existing_models: humanized mice and myeloid limitation
study_aim: one sentence aim + what was tested
introduction.citation_density: one ref per major claim; never stack 3 refs in first sentence

abstract.logic: problem → approach → key quantitative findings → mechanistic conclusion
abstract.mechanism_template: State what the data imply beyond platform utility (e.g. pathway X occurs independently of Y).

results.subsection_pattern: one major figure per subsection H2
per_finding: anchor every number: (Fig. X, panel Y)

discussion.open_template: Lead with the single strongest novel finding in one sentence before context.

quality_rules._note: These override the default Q-rules in draft_section.system.md for this article type.

abstract_bmrc.note: Pattern only — do not copy wording from any specific paper.
abstract_bmrc.Background: 2 sentences: disease/gap + why in vivo human innate immunity is needed.
abstract_bmrc.Results: 3-4 sentences: engraftment + pathway readouts + pharmacology with numbers.
abstract_bmrc.Conclusion: 1-2 sentences: platform competence + one mechanistic insight.

exemplar_patterns.results_sentence: IP LPS increased serum hTNF to ~50 ng/mL (25-fold vs PBS, p<0.001; Fig. 2A).
exemplar_patterns.discussion_opener: The central finding is that human myeloid cells in this model mount separable NF-κB and inflammasome responses amenable to selective pharmacology.
